name: Mushroom Steak (from Aust Meat Corp)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a heavy-based pan over high heat.
2. Brush both sides of the steak with oil.
3. To seal, cook the steak 2-3 minutes on each side until juices appear on the uncooked side.
4. Turn the steak when juices appear.
5. Note: The thickness of the steak determines cooking time. As a guide:
- Rare: Remove after sealing (2-3 minutes total).
- Medium: Reduce heat, add an extra 2-3 minutes on each side.
- Well done: Cook an additional 4-6 minutes on each side.
6. Test the steak's doneness by pressing with tongs:
- Rare feels spongy.
- Medium has some resistance.
- Well done feels firm.
7. Remove the steak from heat and let it rest while making the sauce.
8. Add the onion and mushrooms to any pan juices. Cook for 1 minute.
9. Stir in Worcestershire sauce, beef stock, and parsley until slightly thickened.
10. Serve with steamed vegetables and mashed potatoes.
Recipe from Australian Meat and Live-stock Corporation cards.
I also recommend adding about 2-3 tablespoons of cornflower to the sauce to thicken it slightly.
